issues.title: Coverage: Users: requests (list, create, get, cancel, action, update)
issues.body_raw:
**Domain:** Users
**Priority:** High
**Effort:** M (1–3 days)

## Background
Add coverage for the user requests resource (list, create, get, cancel,
update, perform action). Endpoints and verbs are documented and the
proposed signatures cover the main flows. The audit (2026-05-01, codex
direct) flagged that `cancel_user_request` should make `reason` required
because Alma documents `reason` as a required query parameter on
`DELETE /requests/{id}`.

## Relevant Alma API documentation
- Alma dev-network: https://developers.exlibrisgroup.com/alma/apis/users/
- Endpoints touched:
  - `GET /almaws/v1/users/{user_id}/requests`
  - `POST /almaws/v1/users/{user_id}/requests`
  - `GET /almaws/v1/users/{user_id}/requests/{request_id}`
  - `POST /almaws/v1/users/{user_id}/requests/{request_id}` (`op=...`)
  - `PUT /almaws/v1/users/{user_id}/requests/{request_id}`
  - `DELETE /almaws/v1/users/{user_id}/requests/{request_id}`

Per docs, the cancel endpoint expects a `reason` query param (and an
optional `note`); requests can be canceled but not deleted in the literal
sense — the DELETE verb is the documented "cancel" path.

## Current wrapper behavior
None of the user-requests endpoints are wrapped today.

## Expected wrapper behavior

```python
def list_user_requests(
    self,
    user_id: str,
    limit: int = 100,
    offset: int = 0,
    request_type: str = None,
    status: str = None,
) -> Dict[str, Any]: ...

def create_user_request(
    self,
    user_id: str,
    request_data: Dict[str, Any],
    *,
    mms_id: str = None,
    holding_id: str = None,
    item_pid: str = None,
    user_id_type: str = None,
) -> AlmaResponse:
    """
    Optional query params let callers create a request scoped to a specific
    bib/holding/item without restructuring the body.
    """

def get_user_request(self, user_id: str, request_id: str) -> Dict[str, Any]: ...

def cancel_user_request(
    self,
    user_id: str,
    request_id: str,
    reason: str,            # REQUIRED per Alma docs
    note: str = None,
    notify_user: bool = True,
) -> AlmaResponse: ...

def update_user_request(
    self, user_id: str, request_id: str, request_data: Dict[str, Any],
) -> AlmaResponse: ...

def perform_user_request_action(
    self, user_id: str, request_id: str, op: str,
) -> AlmaResponse: ...
```

## Implementation notes
- Make `reason` a positional required parameter on `cancel_user_request` so
  callers can't accidentally hit Alma's 400 for missing reason. Validate
  non-empty and raise `AlmaValidationError` if blank.
- `op` values for the action POST aren't enumerated on the public docs index;
  document the ones you actually hit during integration tests in the
  docstring.
- The DELETE verb is what Alma documents for "cancel" — wire
  `cancel_user_request` to the DELETE endpoint, not POST `op=cancel`.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] All six methods exposed with type hints and Google-style docstrings.
- [ ] `cancel_user_request(reason)` requires `reason` (non-empty validation)
      and forwards it as a query parameter to DELETE
      `.../requests/{request_id}`.
- [ ] `list_user_requests` supports the documented `limit`, `offset`,
      `request_type`, `status` filters.
- [ ] Unit tests cover: missing `reason` raises `AlmaValidationError`;
      `op` is wired into the action POST query string; `cancel_user_request`
      uses HTTP DELETE not POST.
- [ ] At least one SANDBOX integration test that creates → gets → cancels.
- [ ] No `print()` — all operations logged via `alma_logging`.
- [ ] `scripts/smoke_import.py` and `tests/test_public_api_contract.py` pass.

## Open questions
- Which `op` values on `POST /requests/{id}` are still supported in current
  Alma releases (docs hint at `move`, `prioritize`, etc.) — confirm during
  integration testing and list them in the docstring.

## Audit notes (2026-05-01)
- Primary audit: **Aligned** — all six endpoints and HTTP verbs match docs
  exactly.
- Codex direct audit: **Partially aligned** — flagged that
  `cancel_user_request(reason: str = None)` makes `reason` optional, but
  Alma documents it as required.
- Decision: take the codex finding; promote `reason` to a required
  positional parameter and validate non-empty at call time.

## Files to touch
- `src/almaapitk/domains/users.py` (extend existing `Users` class)
- `tests/unit/domains/test_users.py`

## References
- Alma dev-network: https://developers.exlibrisgroup.com/alma/apis/users/
- Use the `alma-api-expert` skill for endpoint quirks and validation rules

<!-- prereqs:auto:begin -->
## Prerequisites

**Recommended (would simplify or improve this work, but not strictly blocking):**
- #3 — Persistent requests.Session (every new HTTP call inherits connection pooling)
- #4 — Consolidate HTTP verbs into _request() (every new method routes through one chokepoint)
- #5 — Retry with exponential backoff for 429 / 5xx (every new method gets 429/5xx resilience for free)
- #9 — Map Alma error codes to specific exception subclasses (new methods raise typed Alma errors instead of generic AlmaAPIError)
- #10 — Propagate tracking_id and alma_code on errors (errors carry tracking_id for support escalation)
- #14 — Replace print() with logger; remove safe_request() (no print() statements in the new methods)
- #11 — iter_paged() generator at the client level (list/search methods should use iter_paged() instead of manual offset loops)

<!-- prereqs:auto:end -->

## Notes for the implementing agent
- Mirror an existing domain method as the pattern source (e.g., `Acquisitions.get_invoice` for read, `Acquisitions.create_invoice_simple` for write).
- All public methods must have type hints + Google-style docstrings.
- Validate required inputs at top of method and raise `AlmaValidationError` with a clear message.
- Log method entry with key parameters (info), success with result identifiers (info), and errors with full context (error).
- NEVER print — use `self.logger`.
- Test in SANDBOX first. Add at least one unit test under `tests/unit/` and one integration test under `tests/integration/`.
- After implementation, `poetry run python scripts/smoke_import.py` and the public-API contract test MUST pass.

- POST /requests/{id} dispatches via `op` query parameter to actions like `move`, `prioritize`, etc.
- Pair this work with the bib-level/item-level requests issues (#49, #50) for shared validation helpers.

---
Filed as part of the coverage-expansion backlog. See `docs/superpowers/specs/2026-04-30-coverage-expansion-design.md` for context.
